title: Loader Integration

primary_when: request is about plugin loader integration
request involves bepinex or loader startup
must_not_own_when: request is about assembly parsing or UI

solves: attaching a mod runtime to a Unity game through a loader entry point
establishing deterministic startup before patches, modules, UI, and feature systems depend on it
keeping loader-specific code outside feature modules

core_principles: The loader boundary should be thin and deterministic.
Diagnostics and emergency logging should be available before optional systems start.
Loader API details should be hidden behind a toolkit or runtime entry surface.
Feature modules should depend on runtime contracts, not on the loader.
constraints: Do not perform expensive reverse lookup or feature initialization directly in the loader constructor when a later phase is safer.
Do not let feature modules call loader APIs directly.
Do not start patch-heavy systems before diagnostics are configured.
Do not assume shutdown is always called; runtime state should tolerate partial teardown.
Keep mod scope, config paths, and logging context explicit.
decision_rules: Use loader-integration when the task is about plugin entry, attach, bootstrap, or loader lifecycle alignment.
Attach plugin-lifecycle when startup/shutdown sequencing or runtime resource ownership matters.
Attach diagnostics when the runtime must report hook, module, probe, or config failures.
Attach harmony-patching only after the loader can safely initialize and report patch availability.
If multiple mods will consume the toolkit, expose a stable public runtime facade instead of loader-specific entry points.

preferred_patterns: single plugin entry that delegates to a runtime attach method
explicit update, late update, fixed update, GUI, and shutdown forwarding
composition root configured before module initialization
public facade over internal runtime and module systems
avoid_patterns: feature initialization scattered across loader callbacks
global mutable state initialized before logging and config are ready
loader-specific APIs leaking into feature code
implicit startup ordering through static constructors

recipes.name: Create a deterministic loader entry
recipes.when: Use when a Unity mod must attach a reusable runtime/toolkit through a plugin loader.
steps: Keep the plugin entry class minimal and delegate to a runtime attach method.
Configure logging, config paths, and composition root before modules start.
Initialize dispatcher or main-thread bridge before queued runtime work can be scheduled.
Register modules and patches only after diagnostics can record failures.
Forward loader update, fixed update, GUI, and shutdown callbacks explicitly.
Expose health or module state snapshots for runtime verification.

recipes.name: Expose a reusable toolkit attach surface
recipes.when: Use when more than one feature or mod should consume the same runtime foundation.
steps: Move loader-specific details behind a public attach facade.
Resolve mod scope, config paths, logging, and composition root inside the runtime boundary.
Keep feature modules dependent on toolkit contracts rather than loader classes.
Expose lifecycle forwarding methods for update, fixed update, GUI, and shutdown.
Expose diagnostics so consumers can verify attach and module readiness.

pitfalls: Using loader callbacks as the architecture instead of a boundary.
Starting Harmony patches before a failure can be logged.
Depending on shutdown for all cleanup in a loader that may not guarantee it.
Letting every feature create its own config/logging/runtime conventions.
Hiding attach order behind static initialization.
review_checklist: Are diagnostics configured before optional systems?
Can module and hook failures be inspected?
Is feature code isolated from loader details?
Is shutdown or partial teardown handled deliberately?

references.title: Runtime Integration
references.interpretation: Use this first for loader-to-runtime layering and lifecycle boundaries.
references.why: It defines the architecture around attach, modules, patches, probes, events, diagnostics, and facades.

references.title: Diagnostics And Readiness
references.interpretation: Use when loader attach must expose health, module state, queue pressure, or unavailable integrations.
references.why: It defines what a ready or degraded runtime should report.
